title: Sort of Sort
description:
Sorting takes so long... but if we don’t mind losing some data we can sort of sort much faster!

A sort of sorted list is a monotonically increasing list containing all elements of another list $a$ that were originally in sorted order. That is, a sort of sorted list obtained from list $a$ contains all $a_i$ such that $a_i≥a_j$ for all $0≤j<i$.
input: The first line of input contains a single integer $N$, the length of the unsorted list ($1≤N≤100\,000$). The next line contains $N$ space separated integers $a_i$ ($-200\,000≤a_i≤200\,000$).
output: Output a single line of space separated integers representing the sort of sorted list obtained from the given list $a$.
source: , University, University of Alberta Programming Contest, UAPC 2025, Division 1, University, University of Alberta Programming Contest, UAPC 2025, Division 2
